title: Review Specialized Agent Guardrails End To End
description: Inspect the completed implementation, tests, and documentation to confirm guardrails are externally managed, injected into prompts, visible in bead inspection, and enforced through blocked role-scope outcomes without introducing unnecessary framework complexity.
acceptance_criteria: Review confirms all acceptance criteria from the feature spec are satisfied, including prompt injection, external templates, missing-template failure, blocked role-scope outcomes, visibility, and automated coverage.
Review confirms documentation and tests match the implemented behavior and highlight any remaining gaps.
Review does not implement feature work and instead records any defects or follow-up recommendations as review output.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed specs/specialized-agent-guardrails.md, the guardrail templates under templates/agents/, and the implementation in src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py, src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py, src/codex_orchestrator/storage.py, src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py, and src/codex_orchestrator/models.py. Verified the documented behavior in README.md and ran `PYTHONPATH=src python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator`: 38 tests passed. Confirmed prompt injection for multiple agent types, clear missing-template failure, persisted guardrail metadata and prompt context, `bead show` visibility, and blocked role-scope handoff preservation.
handoff_summary.remaining: No review findings were discovered. Scope remains the assigned review bead.
handoff_summary.risks: Residual operational risk remains that ad hoc Python imports without `PYTHONPATH=src` can resolve `codex_orchestrator` from a sibling checkout in this environment. The reviewed feature paths are root-aware and passed in the intended worktree, but downstream agents should validate from the same checkout they mean to inspect.
handoff_summary.next_action: No implementation follow-up is required for this feature based on the reviewed acceptance criteria.
handoff_summary.block_reason: Review reported unresolved findings in remaining.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Agents validating from a different checkout or installed-package path may observe a different imported module tree than the feature worktree. Use the feature worktree directly and prefer `PYTHONPATH=src` for verification to avoid cross-checkout ambiguity.

conflict_risks: Agents validating from a different checkout or installed-package path may observe a different imported module tree than the feature worktree. Use the feature worktree directly and prefer `PYTHONPATH=src` for verification to avoid cross-checkout ambiguity.

execution_history.summary: Review found 1 acceptance-risking defect: guardrail templates are resolved from the imported package location instead of the repository/worktree root. Docs and tests otherwise cover prompt injection, blocked role-scope handoffs, visibility, and missing-template behavior.

Review found no acceptance-blocking defects. Guardrail templates are externally managed, injected from the active execution root, persisted for inspection, exposed through bead JSON, and blocked role-scope outcomes are preserved with automated coverage. Review reported unresolved findings and requires follow-up.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review changed files and call out bugs, regressions, missing tests, and documentation gaps.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.

worker_prompt_context.title: Review Specialized Agent Guardrails End To End
worker_prompt_context.description: Inspect the completed implementation, tests, and documentation to confirm guardrails are externally managed, injected into prompts, visible in bead inspection, and enforced through blocked role-scope outcomes without introducing unnecessary framework complexity.
acceptance_criteria: Review confirms all acceptance criteria from the feature spec are satisfied, including prompt injection, external templates, missing-template failure, blocked role-scope outcomes, visibility, and automated coverage.
Review confirms documentation and tests match the implemented behavior and highlight any remaining gaps.
Review does not implement feature work and instead records any defects or follow-up recommendations as review output.
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: Any downstream agent validating this feature from a different checkout or installed-package path may observe inconsistent guardrail behavior unless they force `PYTHONPATH=src` or otherwise ensure imports come from the same worktree being tested.

handoff_summary.completed: Inspected [specs/specialized-agent-guardrails.md](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/specs/specialized-agent-guardrails.md), the implementation in [prompts.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py#L8), [scheduler.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py#L142), [storage.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/src/codex_orchestrator/storage.py#L136), the templates under [templates/agents](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/templates/agents), the README section in [README.md](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/README.md#L38), and the new coverage in [tests/test_orchestrator.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/tests/test_orchestrator.py#L357). Verified automated coverage with `PYTHONPATH=src python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator` in the feature worktree: 29 tests passed.
handoff_summary.remaining: Finding 1: [prompts.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/worktrees/B0002/src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py#L8) hard-codes `TEMPLATES_DIR` from `Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]`. In this orchestration model, execution happens in shared feature worktrees, but template loading ignores the active repository root and instead reads from whichever checkout supplied the imported package. That breaks the "external editable source of truth" requirement in multi-worktree or mismatched-import setups, can make `bead show` metadata point at the wrong template copy, and can hide missing-template failures if another checkout still has the file. The review worktree reproduced the underlying path-resolution risk: plain `python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator` imported `codex_orchestrator` from a sibling checkout instead of this worktree.
handoff_summary.risks: Until template lookup is root-aware, acceptance criteria 2, 3, and 5 are only conditionally satisfied. Operators can edit templates in the feature worktree while the scheduler loads a different copy, leading to prompt drift and misleading persisted guardrail metadata.
handoff_summary.next_action: Have the developer make guardrail template resolution derive from the active orchestrator repository root/worktree rather than module import location, then add a regression test that exercises template loading against a non-default root.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Any downstream agent validating this feature from a different checkout or installed-package path may observe inconsistent guardrail behavior unless they force `PYTHONPATH=src` or otherwise ensure imports come from the same worktree being tested.

last_agent_result.summary: Review found no acceptance-blocking defects. Guardrail templates are externally managed, injected from the active execution root, persisted for inspection, exposed through bead JSON, and blocked role-scope outcomes are preserved with automated coverage. Review reported unresolved findings and requires follow-up.
